description: Queue Semantics & Frictionless Send/Receive UX. Reshape CopyEverywhere around two ideas: (1) the relay server is a message queue — a clip is a message, send produces, receive consumes, consumed clips are deleted, and the App panel shows the live server queue with no local history; (2) devices can be addressed — clients register as named devices and a sender can target a specific device, in which case the server SSE-pushes the clip and the receiver auto-applies it (text/image to clipboard, file to Downloads). On top of that, drag-and-drop (files or text) onto the menu bar icon / floating ball is the one-gesture send path, and the OS paste shortcut (⌘V / Ctrl+V) sends the current clipboard while the panel is focused.

userStories.title: Server: device registration table and endpoint
userStories.description: As a client, I need to register myself with the server so other clients can target me.
acceptanceCriteria: New devices table: id (TEXT PK, 8-char alphanumeric, server-generated), name (TEXT), platform (TEXT: macos|windows|linux), last_seen_at (DATETIME), created_at (DATETIME)
POST /api/v1/devices/register body {name, platform} returns {device_id}; idempotent on (name, platform) — re-registering returns the existing id and bumps last_seen_at
GET /api/v1/devices returns list of {device_id, name, platform, last_seen_at} for all devices seen in the last 30 days
db.Open still uses SetMaxOpenConns(1) (single-writer rule)
Handler tests use t.TempDir() and gin.New() per existing convention
go test ./... passes
go build succeeds with no errors

userStories.title: Server: add target_device_id and consumed_at to clips
userStories.description: As a developer, I need clips to optionally carry a target device and to track consumption so the queue model works.
acceptanceCriteria: Add columns to clips: target_device_id (TEXT nullable, soft FK to devices.id), sender_device_id (TEXT nullable), consumed_at (DATETIME nullable)
POST /api/v1/clips accepts optional multipart fields target_device_id and sender_device_id and persists them
POST /api/v1/uploads/init accepts the same two optional fields and they are persisted on the resulting clip
Migration is in-place: existing rows get NULL in the new columns and continue to work
go test ./... passes
go build succeeds with no errors

userStories.title: Server: queue listing and atomic consume-and-delete
userStories.description: As a client, I need to see what is in the queue and to consume an item exactly once so two devices cannot both grab the same clip.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/v1/clips?device_id=XXX returns clips where consumed_at IS NULL AND (target_device_id IS NULL OR target_device_id = XXX), newest first, using the existing trimmed clipResponse shape

GET /api/v1/clips/:id/raw is now an atomic claim: UPDATE clips SET consumed_at = now() WHERE id = ? AND consumed_at IS NULL; if RowsAffected == 0 return 410 Gone with {error: "already_consumed"}; otherwise stream the body
After a successful raw fetch, the on-disk file and the row are deleted inline (after the response writer flushes) so disk frees immediately
GET /api/v1/clips/latest is deprecated but still returns 200 for one release with a Deprecation header
Race test: two parallel GET /:id/raw calls — exactly one gets the body, the other gets 410
go test ./... passes
go build succeeds with no errors

userStories.title: Server: SSE push channel for targeted clips
userStories.description: As a targeted-receive client, I need a long-lived connection that tells me the moment a clip addressed to me lands, so reception is instant and I do not have to poll.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/v1/devices/:id/stream opens a Server-Sent Events stream (Content-Type: text/event-stream), authenticated by the existing Bearer middleware

When a clip with target_device_id == :id becomes ready (single-shot upload finished, or chunked upload complete succeeds), the server emits an SSE event named 'clip' with payload {clip_id, type, filename, size_bytes}
Untargeted clips (target_device_id IS NULL) do NOT push — they wait in the queue for any client to pull
Connection sends a heartbeat comment line every 25 seconds and supports clean reconnect (Last-Event-ID header accepted but optional)
If multiple SSE connections exist for the same device id, all of them receive the event (in-memory fan-out via a sync.RWMutex-protected subscriber map)
go test ./... passes (use httptest.NewRecorder + flusher)
go build succeeds with no errors

userStories.title: Server: cleanup loop tweak and CLEANUP_INTERVAL_SECONDS env
userStories.description: As an operator, I want consumed clips and stale unconsumed clips to be cleaned up on a configurable cadence so the relay does not accumulate junk.
acceptanceCriteria: cleanup/ goroutine deletes any clip where consumed_at IS NOT NULL and consumed_at < now() - 60s (covers any inline-delete edge case from US-020)
Existing TTL behavior for unconsumed clips is unchanged
Cleanup interval configurable via env CLEANUP_INTERVAL_SECONDS (default 30)
go test ./... passes
go build succeeds with no errors

userStories.title: macOS: device registration on first launch
userStories.description: As a macOS user, I want the App to register itself with the relay the first time I configure a token, so I can be targeted by other devices.
acceptanceCriteria: On successful save in ConfigView, call POST /devices/register with {name: Host.current().localizedName ?? "Mac", platform: "macos"}
Resulting device_id is persisted in ConfigStore (UserDefaults; not a secret)
ConfigView shows the registered device name and id (read-only) under the token field
Re-registering on subsequent launches is a no-op (server-side idempotence handles it) but still bumps last_seen_at
swift build succeeds

userStories.title: macOS: drag-and-drop file or text onto menu bar icon
userStories.description: As a macOS user, I want to drag a file from Finder or a text selection from any app onto the menu bar icon and have it upload immediately, with no panel open.
acceptanceCriteria: The menu bar item accepts both NSPasteboard.PasteboardType.fileURL and .string drops, via a custom NSView with registerForDraggedTypes([.fileURL, .string]) hosted on NSStatusItem.button
Dropping one or more files starts upload(s) — small files (< 50 MB) via POST /clips, ≥ 50 MB via the chunked flow, reusing ApiClient
Dropping a text selection sends it as a text clip via POST /clips
During the drop hover, the menu bar icon shows a visual highlight
On success, a UserNotifications toast says 'Sent <filename>' or 'Sent text (<N> chars)'
On failure, a toast says 'Failed to send: <reason>'
swift build succeeds

userStories.title: macOS: drag-and-drop file or text onto main panel
userStories.description: As a macOS user, I want the open panel to also accept dropped files and text, so I do not have to close it and aim for the menu bar.
acceptanceCriteria: MainPanelView has a .onDrop(of: [.fileURL, .text], ...) modifier covering its full bounds
Files trigger the file send path; text triggers the text send path — single shared SendService with US-024
During hover, the panel shows a dashed-border overlay 'Drop to send'
swift build succeeds

userStories.title: macOS: ⌘V while panel open sends current clipboard
userStories.description: As a macOS user, when the panel is already open I want to press ⌘V (the OS paste shortcut) and have whatever is on my clipboard sent to the relay, so I do not have to learn a new shortcut.
acceptanceCriteria: While the MenuBarExtra panel is the key window, ⌘V is intercepted and triggers a send (e.g. via NSEvent.addLocalMonitorForEvents on .keyDown, or a hidden button with .keyboardShortcut("v", modifiers: .command))
Send picks the first usable representation in priority order: text → image → file URL (file URL becomes a file send)
If the clipboard is empty, show a toast 'Clipboard is empty' and do nothing
After a successful send, show an in-panel toast banner: 'Sent: <preview>'
No confirmation dialog
⌘V should not paste into any text input that happens to be focused inside the panel — the send hijacks the shortcut globally while the panel is the key window
swift build succeeds

userStories.title: macOS: panel shows live server queue and HistoryStore is removed
userStories.description: As a macOS user, I want the panel to show what is currently waiting on the server, so 'history' and 'queue' are the same thing.
acceptanceCriteria: MainPanelView fetches GET /clips?device_id=<self> on open and on a 5s refresh tick while open
Each row shows: type icon, filename or text preview (first 60 chars), size, age, and a 'Receive' button
'Receive' calls GET /clips/:id/raw and writes text/image to NSPasteboard.general or saves a file to ~/Downloads/; after success the row disappears (server has deleted it)
Empty state: 'Queue is empty — copy something and click the icon.'
Delete macos/CopyEverywhere/Sources/CopyEverywhere/HistoryStore.swift and remove all references from views and ConfigStore wiring
swift build succeeds

userStories.title: macOS: target-device selector and SSE auto-receive
userStories.description: As a macOS user, I want to optionally pin a target device so my sends bypass the queue and land directly on that device — and conversely, when someone targets me, the clip lands in my clipboard with no clicks.
acceptanceCriteria: ConfigView has a 'Target device' picker populated from GET /devices (excluding self); first option is '(Queue — any device)'
When a target is set, all sends (drag-drop, ⌘V-while-panel-open, manual file picker) include target_device_id in the request
Whenever a token is configured, the App opens a long-lived SSE connection to /devices/<self>/stream

On a 'clip' SSE event, the App calls GET /clips/:id/raw and: text → write to NSPasteboard.general with toast 'Received text from <sender>'; image → write to NSPasteboard.general with toast 'Received image from <sender>'; file → save to ~/Downloads/<filename> with toast 'Saved <filename> to Downloads'
SSE reconnects with exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s, capped at 30s) on disconnect
swift build succeeds

userStories.title: Windows: device registration on first launch
userStories.description: As a Windows user, I want the App to register itself with the relay the first time I configure a token, so I can be targeted by other devices.
acceptanceCriteria: On token save in ConfigWindow, call POST /devices/register with {name: Environment.MachineName, platform: "windows"}
device_id persisted in ConfigStore (JSON in %LOCALAPPDATA%\CopyEverywhere\config.json)
Config UI shows device name and id read-only
Re-registering on subsequent launches is a no-op
dotnet build succeeds

userStories.title: Windows: drag-and-drop file or text onto floating ball
userStories.description: As a Windows user, I want to drag a file or selected text onto a small drop target. Because Hardcodet.NotifyIcon.Wpf does not support drop targets on the tray icon, we ship an always-on-top floating ball window instead.
acceptanceCriteria: Add FloatingBallWindow: 64x64 borderless circular Window with Topmost=true, WindowStyle=None, AllowsTransparency=true, AllowDrop=true, draggable to reposition (position persisted in config)
A 'Show floating ball' toggle in config (default ON for Windows) controls visibility
Drop handler accepts both DataFormats.FileDrop and DataFormats.UnicodeText (fall back to DataFormats.Text)
Files trigger the existing ApiClient send path — small files via POST /clips, ≥ 50 MB via chunked upload reusing ProgressStreamContent
Text triggers a text clip send via POST /clips
Success / failure surfaced via Microsoft.Toolkit.Uwp.Notifications toast
dotnet build succeeds

userStories.title: Windows: drag-and-drop file or text onto main window
userStories.description: As a Windows user, the main window also accepts drops — both files and text — so I do not have to use the floating ball.
acceptanceCriteria: MainWindow root grid has AllowDrop=true and handles Drop / DragEnter for both DataFormats.FileDrop and text formats
Same upload path as US-030 (single shared SendService)
Visual hover state shows a 'Drop to send' overlay
dotnet build succeeds

userStories.title: Windows: Ctrl+V while main window focused sends current clipboard
userStories.description: As a Windows user, while the main window is focused I want to press Ctrl+V (the OS paste shortcut) and have the current clipboard sent — no new shortcut to learn.
acceptanceCriteria: Bind a KeyBinding for Ctrl+V on MainWindow (or a RoutedCommand on the window's InputBindings) so the shortcut fires only while the window is focused
Send picks the first usable representation in priority order: text (Clipboard.GetText) → image (Clipboard.GetImage) → file URL (Clipboard.GetFileDropList)
If the clipboard is empty, show a toast 'Clipboard is empty' and do nothing
After a successful send, show an in-window toast banner: 'Sent: <preview>'
No confirmation dialog
Ctrl+V should not paste into any text input that happens to be focused inside the window — the binding wins
dotnet build succeeds

userStories.title: Windows: main window shows live server queue and HistoryStore is removed
userStories.description: As a Windows user, I want the main window to show what is currently waiting on the server, so 'history' and 'queue' are the same thing.
acceptanceCriteria: MainWindow polls GET /clips?device_id=<self> on open and every 5s while visible
Each row: type icon, preview, size, age, 'Receive' button
'Receive' calls GET /clips/:id/raw and writes text/image to Clipboard, or saves a file to the user's Downloads folder via Environment.GetFolderPath(SpecialFolder.UserProfile) + "\\Downloads"
After a successful receive, the row disappears
Delete windows/CopyEverywhere/Services/HistoryStore.cs and remove all references
dotnet build succeeds

userStories.title: Windows: target-device selector and SSE auto-receive
userStories.description: As a Windows user, I want to optionally pin a target device so my sends bypass the queue, and conversely, when someone targets me, the clip lands in my clipboard with no clicks.
acceptanceCriteria: Config UI has a 'Target device' combobox populated from GET /devices (excluding self); first option is '(Queue — any device)'
When a target is set, all sends include target_device_id in the request

While a token is configured, a background SSE reader is open to /devices/<self>/stream — implemented with HttpClient using HttpCompletionOption.ResponseHeadersRead and a StreamReader line loop (no third-party SSE client required)
On a 'clip' SSE event: text/image → Clipboard, file → Downloads folder, with a toast naming the sender
Reconnect with exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s, capped at 30s)
dotnet build succeeds
